content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Fix resource leak in shell completion: wrap _resolve_context's make_context calls (cli.make_context, cmd.make_context for non-chained and chained groups) in 'with' statements so Context.__exit__/close() runs and file handles (click.File) get closed. May touch core.py only if Context/Command internals need a small supporting tweak (likely no functional change needed there, TBD).

fields.note: Fix resource leak in shell completion: wrap _resolve_context's make_context calls (cli.make_context, cmd.make_context for non-chained and chained groups) in 'with' statements so Context.__exit__/close() runs and file handles (click.File) get closed. May touch core.py only if Context/Command internals need a small supporting tweak (likely no functional change needed there, TBD).

content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: I'll own shell_completion.py: wrap the 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context with 'with ... as ctx:' blocks (main cli.make_context, non-chained cmd.make_context, chained cmd.make_context) so Context.__exit__ closes file handles. I'll also apply your validate_nesting=False requirement at those same call sites - please send a SPEC confirming exact call signature/kwarg needed (e.g. cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True, validate_nesting=False)) and any other shell_completion.py call sites that need it.

fields.note: I'll own shell_completion.py: wrap the 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context with 'with ... as ctx:' blocks (main cli.make_context, non-chained cmd.make_context, chained cmd.make_context) so Context.__exit__ closes file handles. I'll also apply your validate_nesting=False requirement at those same call sites - please send a SPEC confirming exact call signature/kwarg needed (e.g. cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True, validate_nesting=False)) and any other shell_completion.py call sites that need it.

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/core.py
spec: My fix (closing file resources during shell completion) requires NO functional changes to core.py - Context already fully supports the context manager protocol (__enter__/__exit__/close()) needed for 'with ctx:' blocks in shell_completion.py to work correctly. You own core.py entirely for your validate_nesting feature. One ask: please make sure validate_nesting defaults preserve current behavior for normal (non-completion) code paths, since I'm not modifying core.py at all.
note: No requirements from my feature in core.py; deferring full ownership to you

content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
spec: Ack: In _resolve_context, I will wrap the three make_context call sites in 'with ... as ctx:' blocks AND explicitly pass validate_nesting=False to each: (1) cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args, validate_nesting=False), (2) cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True, validate_nesting=False) for non-chained groups, (3) cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True, validate_nesting=False) for chained groups. I'll grep shell_completion.py for any other Context/make_context construction sites and apply the same.
note: Acknowledged, proceeding to implement in shell_completion.py only

content:
type: DONE
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implemented: wrapped all 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context with 'with ... as ctx:' blocks (closes Context on exit, fixing File resource leak during shell completion) and passed validate_nesting=False at each per your spec. Verified via monkeypatched Context accepting validate_nesting: ResourceWarning eliminated, full test suite (558 passed) and shell_completion tests (40 passed) green. Not touching core.py, per our agreement.
